descripcion:
Micron is using automakers to hedge the next memory bust.

Ford and GM are locking in supply while memory is scarce and expensive.

But chip shortages do not last forever. Capacity expands, supply catches up, and commodity pricing eventually breaks.

So the automakers get very expensive certainty.

Micron gets customers committed before the downturn arrives.

We published how we’re trading the memory cycle. Comment LETTER to get the research.

descripcion:
0DTE options expire the same day, which makes them extremely sensitive to small moves in the S&P.

When enough traders crowd into the same strikes, market makers have to hedge with futures.

At enough size, that hedge can push the index higher, lower, or pin it near a level.

The option may cost pennies.

The hedge behind it can move billions.

Comment LETTER and I’ll send you the Crown Macro Letter.

descripcion:
The copper cowboys are at it again.

The global copper shortage may be manufactured by a group of crazy traders.

They bought refined copper overseas, rushed it into the U.S. before a possible tariff, and parked it in warehouses.

That trade pushed U.S. stocks from roughly 80,000 to 650,000 tonnes.

If tariffs hit, they win.

If not, all that copper has to move at bargain prices.

Long live the copper cowboys.

descripcion:
Macro fund traders cover a lot of ground for one reason. They are trying to avoid overtrading a small area.

The number of things you watch is the number of real opportunities you get.

Four indicators on a couple of tickers produces one or two authentic trades a year. Either you wait twelve months for the next clean setup, or you force trades that are not there.

Most people force it and end up losing money.

The fix is breadth. Cross-asset pairs, calendar spreads, breadth ratios, funding indicators.

Each one is its own opportunity surface, and most are dormant until they suddenly are not. More coverage means you never have to manufacture that isn’t there.

Comment LETTER for this week's Crown Macro Letter.

descripcion:
- The Pareto Principle 👇🏻

(aka the 80/20 Rule)

20% of your efforts, produce 80% of your results…

=====

What this means for traders:

• 20% of your trades, make 80% of your PROFITS.

• 20% of your set-ups, make 80% of your PROGRESS.

• 20% of the concepts you use, make 80% of your WINS.

and conversely…
• 20% of your mistakes, make 80% of your LOSSES!

Pay attention to your smallest leaks, that make the majority of your progress 🫡

#daytrading #tradingpsychology #tradingmindset #forex #futurestrading

👉🏻 Daily Trading Lessons on my STORIES
🚨 @alpha_futures → Code “ABE” for Best Prices
descripcion:
- This is how I do it 👇🏻

HIGHER TIMEFRAME
(h4 - Daily)
- Check once, before you do any other analysis
- Use this to find your direction, your daily bias, your zones

MIDDLE TIMEFRAME
(m15-h1)
- Use this timeframe once you start your trading session
- Find the important levels that align with your daily bias
- Reversal points, breakout levels
- Once price reaches it, confirm on the lower timeframe

LOWER TIMEFRAME
(m1-m5)
- Only look at this once you are at your key level / zone
- Confirm your entry with IFVGs, EMA, BoS etc.
- Any confirmation will do, so long as you keep it consistent
- Most of the hard work is already done on higher timeframe
- Use this to manage positions as well

👉🏻 FOLLOW FOR MORE!
